title: Bidaria indica
intro: Bidaria indica, also known as Bidara or Khair, is a tree species belonging to the family Celastraceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidaria indica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer activities. Bidaria indica is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory infections.
